diagrams.title: Algorithm as Black Box Model
diagrams.description: Input-output transformation showing algorithm as a processing unit
elements: Input Arrow → [Algorithm Box: Finite Step Sequence] → Output Arrow
Box interior shows: Step 1 → Step 2 → ... → Step n
diagrams.purpose: Reinforces the fundamental definition: algorithms transform input to output through finite steps. Visualizes the transformation view that underlies algorithm analysis.

diagrams.title: Five Algorithm Characteristics (FIDOE)
diagrams.description: Pentagon diagram showing the five essential properties in circular relationship
elements: Pentagon vertices: Finiteness (top), Definiteness (upper right), Input (lower right), Output (lower left), Effectiveness (upper left)
Arrows showing cycle: Finiteness → Definiteness → Input → Output → Effectiveness → (loop back)
Each vertex has brief label explaining the property
diagrams.purpose: Memory aid for FIDOE properties. Shows interconnected nature—all five must hold simultaneously for a valid algorithm.

diagrams.title: Algorithm-Program-Process Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Three-layer diagram showing transformation from abstract to concrete to execution
elements: Top layer: ALGORITHM (label: Abstract specification, language-independent)
Middle layer: PROGRAM (label: Concrete code in specific language)
Bottom layer: PROCESS (label: Runtime with CPU, memory, I/O resources)
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the crucial distinction between algorithm (concept), program (implementation), and process (execution)—foundational for understanding what we analyze for complexity.
